landmark_discoveries.finding: Core signal: 53.8%F overall, 96K signals, 3,441 drugs
Severity gradient: fatal AEs MORE female-biased
Drug class spectrum: GLP-1 24.6%F → Antiplatelet 82.8%F
Opioid paradox: 75.7%F despite male-dominated prescribing
Anti-regression phenomenon: effect sizes INCREASE with confirmation
Temporal stability: signal direction constant across 20 years
Validation: 82.8% directional precision across 40 benchmarks
SOC atlas: haematological 71.5%F, ocular 39.6%F most polarized
Reproductive paradox: breast cancer drugs 82.1%F even for breast-specific AEs
FDA BBW drugs: +6.8pp more female-biased (p=5.6e-57)
Centennial: 100 waves, 69 JSONs, 279 figures complete
Disease triangulation: 1,084 diseases with sex-biased drug safety
Herb-drug: 873 toxic NPs mapped to sex-biased drug targets
External DB: CTD+NPASS+LOTUS integration (577K compounds)
Polypharmacy: AE concordance 62.6% across drug pairs
Confidence gradient: 51.4%F → 62.2%F with higher confidence
Report volume: D1=42.2%F → D10=82.5%F (most reported=most female)
Asymmetry: 87.2% of female signals are genuine F excess
Pathway atlas: 328 Reactome pathways, STAT3/ALK 81.4%F
PPI hub drugs: +3.4pp more female (hub→%F ρ=0.118)
Signal stability: cross-tier ρ=0.646, 53.8→67.0%F with data
43 SDE pharmacogenes overlap drug targets (CYP3A4, ESR1, AR)
MoA atlas: Activators 58.8%F, Agonists 41.4%F, GLP1R most male
Class interaction: CPI×Biologic most discordant (ρ=-0.110)
Entropy: low-entropy drugs skew male (D1=31.2%F)
